checks.name: schema_completeness
checks.detail: All required payload fields present. Has customSections for runbook_schema and alertmanager_payload providing operational context.

checks.name: flow_integrity
checks.detail: 3 flows (Alert with Runbook, No Runbook Escalation, Alert Resolution). Flow 1 has n6 connector labeled as 'notion' but should be 'diagnostics' — minor attribution issue. Flow 2 includes error node for Notion API failure.

checks.name: trigger_validity
checks.detail: Webhook trigger has path (/alertmanager), method (POST), and content_type. Well-configured for Alertmanager integration.

checks.name: connector_role_alignment
checks.detail: slack->chat_messaging, github->source_control, notion->knowledge_base all valid roles in connectorRoles.ts

checks.name: adoption_question_fitness
checks.detail: 5 adoption_questions added: notion_runbook_db_id, github_incident_repo, slack_incident_channel, oncall_slack_user, alertmanager_url

checks.name: default_value_coverage
checks.detail: Notion needs database_id (no default possible). GitHub owner/repo required. Slack needs bot_token. All fields have helpText.

checks.name: prompt_quality
checks.detail: Identity: 120 words (excellent depth). Instructions: ~1100 words with 9 numbered steps. Tool guidance: 4 service APIs detailed. 3 examples covering auto-resolve, critical escalation, and no-runbook. Comprehensive error handling.

checks.name: safety_scan
checks.detail: 0 critical. Malformed payloads get fallback handling. INCONCLUSIVE results escalate rather than hide. No destructive auto-actions.

dimensions.name: Prompt Completeness
dimensions.rationale: Identity 120 words with SRE philosophy. Instructions 1100+ words with 9 detailed steps covering the full incident lifecycle. Tool guidance for Notion, Slack, GitHub, and diagnostic endpoints. 3 excellent examples with different outcomes. Custom sections document expected runbook schema and Alertmanager payload format.

dimensions.name: Tool-Prompt Alignment
dimensions.rationale: http_request, file_write, file_read all documented. Notion database query, Slack Block Kit, GitHub issue CRUD all covered with specific endpoints and body examples. Minor: diagnostic HTTP endpoints are dynamic (from runbooks), not hardcoded — this is correct but means the LLM must interpret runbook content.

dimensions.name: Trigger Coherence
dimensions.rationale: Single webhook trigger perfectly matches the use case: Alertmanager sends POST webhooks. Config includes path, method, and content_type. No unnecessary triggers.

dimensions.name: Connector Coverage
dimensions.rationale: Slack, GitHub, Notion all have full connector definitions. Notion connector includes the runbook_database_id field which is essential. All credential fields have detailed setup instructions.

dimensions.name: Memory Design
dimensions.rationale: Agent memory stores incident summaries and outcomes. Notion runbook updated with Last Incident date. Could benefit from more explicit local state tracking (incident log, diagnostic result cache).

dimensions.name: Error Handling
dimensions.rationale: Exceptional: malformed payloads get fallback Slack notification. Notion failure treated as no-runbook with immediate escalation. Diagnostic failures marked INCONCLUSIVE (not false positive). Slack rate limiting with retry. GitHub failure writes local fallback file. Resolved alert without matching issue handled gracefully.

dimensions.name: Use Case Fidelity
dimensions.rationale: All 3 flows covered by prompt. Flow 1 covers the full diagnostic path. Flow 2 handles missing runbooks. Flow 3 handles resolution. Minor: Flow 1 node n6 is labeled as connector 'notion' but represents diagnostic HTTP checks to service endpoints, not Notion API calls.

dimensions.name: Value Clarity
dimensions.rationale: Crystal clear: every alert gets immediate, documented response with diagnostics. Eliminates the gap between alert and first-response. Ensures runbook compliance. Creates trackable incident records automatically.

dimensions.name: Execution Feasibility
dimensions.rationale: Notion, Slack, GitHub APIs are standard. The dynamic diagnostic HTTP checks (endpoints defined in Notion runbooks) require the LLM to interpret and execute arbitrary HTTP calls — feasible but adds complexity. File write for fallback is straightforward.

dimensions.name: Differentiation
dimensions.rationale: Excellent: intelligent runbook matching, automated diagnostics execution, evidence-based incident reports, full lifecycle tracking (fire → diagnose → notify → create issue → resolve → close). No simple automation could replicate this reasoning chain.

dimensions.name: Variable Necessity
dimensions.rationale: Full_prompt_markdown references configuration variables (NOTION_RUNBOOK_DB_ID, GITHUB_OWNER, GITHUB_REPO, DEFAULT_SLACK_CHANNEL, ONCALL_SLACK_USER) but these are not defined as formal template variables. They exist in connector fields and adoption_questions instead, which works.

dimensions.name: Default Quality
dimensions.rationale: Slack channel defaults to #sre-incidents. GitHub repo defaults to 'incidents'. But Notion database ID, GitHub owner, and Alertmanager URL must be configured. Requires moderate setup effort.

issues: No adoption_questions — Notion DB ID, GitHub repo, Slack channel, on-call user, and Alertmanager URL should be asked
Flow 1 node n6 has connector='notion' but represents diagnostic HTTP checks to arbitrary service endpoints, not Notion API calls
Configuration variables referenced in full_prompt_markdown (NOTION_RUNBOOK_DB_ID, GITHUB_OWNER, etc.) not formalized as template variables

template: Added 5 adoption_questions: notion_runbook_db_id, github_incident_repo, slack_incident_channel, oncall_slack_user, alertmanager_url
